name: Asian Summer Rolls
recipeIngredient: 1 2 ounces rice vermicelli (bun), available in some supermarkets and specialty asian food shops
8 rounds of rice paper (banh trang), available in some supermarkets and specialty asian food stores
8 large boston or bibb lettuce leaves, cut in half
8 large shrimp - peeled, deveined, blanched for 1 minute, drained, and cut in half lengthwise
16 large mint leaves, washed and patted dry
1/4 teaspoon hot chili paste or red pepper flakes, to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Soak the rice vermicelli in warm water to cover for 15 to 20 minutes and drain.
2. In a large pot of boiling water, cook the noodles for 2 minutes or until al dente, stirring to separate the strands. Drain, rinse with cold water, and shake dry in a colander.
3. Fill a small shallow dish with warm water. Immerse one sheet of rice paper in the water and let stand until pliable, about 45 seconds. Transfer rice paper to paper towels to drain.
4. On the work surface, lay one piece of lettuce over the bottom third of the rice paper. Arrange 1 tablespoon of the rice noodles, 1 tablespoon of the shredded carrot, some bean sprouts, and 2 mint leaves end to end on the lettuce leaf.
5. Roll up the paper halfway into a cylinder and fold both sides of the paper over the filling.
6. Lay 2 shrimp halves, cut side down and end to end, along the crease. Place several coriander leaves alongside the shrimp.
7. Continue to roll the rice paper into a cylinder, pressing the edge against the cylinder to seal.
8. Place the roll, seam side down, on a plate and cover with a damp towel. Make more summer rolls using the same method with remaining ingredients.
9. In a small saucepan, heat oil over moderately high heat. Cook garlic and chili paste for 1 minute.
10. Add tomato paste, peanut butter, hoisin sauce, sugar, and water to the saucepan, whisking until smooth. Simmer for 2 minutes or until heated through.
11. If necessary, add additional water to thin the sauce to the desired consistency. Pour the sauce into a dipping bowl and sprinkle with chopped peanuts.
12. Serve summer rolls with the dipping sauce.
Yield: 8 summer rolls, 4 servings
